graph.theorem_title: Harmonic functions minimize Dirichlet energy among competitors with the same boundary values
graph.theorem_statement:
Harmonic functions minimize the Dirichlet energy among competitors with the same boundary values.

Let \(\Omega \subset \mathbb{R}^d\) be a bounded domain with sufficiently regular boundary, and let \(g\) be prescribed boundary data. Suppose \(u\) is a sufficiently regular function such that:
- \(u=g\) on \(\partial\Omega\),
- and \(u\) is weakly harmonic, meaning
\[
\int_\Omega \nabla u \cdot \nabla \varphi \, dx = 0
\qquad \text{for every } \varphi \in H_0^1(\Omega).
\]

Then for every sufficiently regular \(v\) with the same boundary data \(v=g\) on \(\partial\Omega\), one has
\[
\int_\Omega |\nabla u|^2\,dx \le \int_\Omega |\nabla v|^2\,dx.
\]
Moreover,
\[
\int_\Omega |\nabla v|^2\,dx
=
\int_\Omega |\nabla u|^2\,dx
+
\int_\Omega |\nabla(v-u)|^2\,dx.
\]

nodes.title: Harmonic functions minimize Dirichlet energy among competitors with the same boundary values
nodes.informal_statement:
If \(u\) is weakly harmonic in \(\Omega\) and \(u=g\) on \(\partial\Omega\), then for every sufficiently regular \(v\) with \(v=g\) on \(\partial\Omega\),
\[
\int_\Omega |\nabla v|^2\,dx
=
\int_\Omega |\nabla u|^2\,dx
+
\int_\Omega |\nabla(v-u)|^2\,dx,
\]
and hence
\[
\int_\Omega |\nabla u|^2\,dx \le \int_\Omega |\nabla v|^2\,dx.
\]
nodes.informal_proof_text:
Fix a competitor \(v\) with the same boundary data and set \(w:=v-u\). By the common boundary condition, \(w=0\) on \(\partial\Omega\), so \(w\in H_0^1(\Omega)\). Apply the energy decomposition from the local claim to obtain
\[
\int_\Omega |\nabla v|^2\,dx
=
\int_\Omega |\nabla u|^2\,dx
+
\int_\Omega |\nabla(v-u)|^2\,dx.
\]
Since the last term is nonnegative, the minimizing inequality follows immediately. Thus \(u\) minimizes the Dirichlet energy among all competitors with the same boundary values.
nodes.formalization_rationale: Natural end-to-end theorem statement once the energy identity is available; formalization is straightforward from nonnegativity of the final term.

nodes.title: Energy decomposition using the weak harmonicity test \(\varphi=v-u\)
nodes.informal_statement:
For \(w:=v-u\), if \(u\) and \(v\) have the same boundary values, then \(w\in H_0^1(\Omega)\) and
\[
\int_\Omega |\nabla v|^2\,dx
=
\int_\Omega |\nabla u|^2\,dx
+
\int_\Omega |\nabla w|^2\,dx.
\]
nodes.informal_proof_text:
Set \(w:=v-u\). Since \(u=v=g\) on \(\partial\Omega\), we have \(w=0\) on \(\partial\Omega\), hence \(w\in H_0^1(\Omega)\). Also \(
abla v=
abla(u+w)=
abla u+
abla w\), so pointwise
\[
|
abla v|^2=|
abla u+
abla w|^2=|
abla u|^2+2
abla u\cdot
abla w+|
abla w|^2.
\]
Integrating gives
\[
\int_\Omega |
abla v|^2\,dx
=
\int_\Omega |
abla u|^2\,dx
+2\int_\Omega 
abla u\cdot
abla w\,dx
+\int_\Omega |
abla w|^2\,dx.
\]
Because \(w\in H_0^1(\Omega)\), it is an admissible test function in the weak harmonicity condition for \(u\), so
\[
\int_\Omega 
abla u\cdot
abla w\,dx=0.
\]
Therefore the cross term vanishes and the stated identity follows. Replacing \(w\) by \(v-u\) yields the advertised form.

nodes.title: Certified local core for Energy decomposition using the weak harmonicity test \(\varphi=v-u\)
nodes.informal_statement: Lean verifies a narrower concrete local core supporting this parent step. See the attached Lean statement and code for the exact certified sublemma.
nodes.informal_proof_text: This node records a verified supporting sublemma extracted from the formalization of parent node 'n_energy_split'. It certifies part of the parent's local proof burden without claiming to cover the full informal node.

formal_artifact.lean_statement:
theorem n_energy_split
    (d : ℕ)
    (Ω : Set (EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin d)))
    (grad_u grad_w : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin d) → EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin d))
    (hu : Integrable (fun x => ‖grad_u x‖ ^ 2) (volume.restrict Ω))
    (hw : Integrable (fun x => ‖grad_w x‖ ^ 2) (volume.restrict Ω))
    (huw : Integrable (fun x => @inner ℝ _ _ (grad_u x) (grad_w x)) (volume.restrict Ω))
    (horth : ∫ x, @inner ℝ _ _ (grad_u x) (grad_w x) ∂(volume.restrict Ω) = 0) :
    (∫ x, ‖grad_u x + grad_w x‖ ^ 2 ∂(volume.restrict Ω)) =
      (∫ x, ‖grad_u x‖ ^ 2 ∂(volume.restrict Ω)) +
        ∫ x, ‖grad_w x‖ ^ 2 ∂(volume.restrict Ω)
formal_artifact.lean_code:
import Mathlib.MeasureTheory.Integral.Bochner.Basic
import Mathlib.Analysis.InnerProductSpace.Basic
import Mathlib.Analysis.InnerProductSpace.PiL2
import Mathlib.MeasureTheory.Measure.Haar.InnerProductSpace

open MeasureTheory

namespace FormalIslands

theorem n_energy_split
    (d : ℕ)
    (Ω : Set (EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin d)))
    (grad_u grad_w : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin d) → EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin d))
    (hu : Integrable (fun x => ‖grad_u x‖ ^ 2) (volume.restrict Ω))
    (hw : Integrable (fun x => ‖grad_w x‖ ^ 2) (volume.restrict Ω))
    (huw : Integrable (fun x => @inner ℝ _ _ (grad_u x) (grad_w x)) (volume.restrict Ω))
    (horth : ∫ x, @inner ℝ _ _ (grad_u x) (grad_w x) ∂(volume.restrict Ω) = 0) :
    (∫ x, ‖grad_u x + grad_w x‖ ^ 2 ∂(volume.restrict Ω)) =
      (∫ x, ‖grad_u x‖ ^ 2 ∂(volume.restrict Ω)) +
        ∫ x, ‖grad_w x‖ ^ 2 ∂(volume.restrict Ω) := by
  have h_expand :
      (fun x => ‖grad_u x + grad_w x‖ ^ 2) =
        fun x => ‖grad_u x‖ ^ 2 + 2 * @inner ℝ _ _ (grad_u x) (grad_w x) + ‖grad_w x‖ ^ 2 := by
    funext x
    simpa using norm_add_sq_real (grad_u x) (grad_w x)
  rw [h_expand]
  have h_two_inner :
      Integrable (fun x => 2 * @inner ℝ _ _ (grad_u x) (grad_w x)) (volume.restrict Ω) :=
    huw.const_mul (2 : ℝ)
  have h_first_two :
      Integrable (fun x => ‖grad_u x‖ ^ 2 + 2 * @inner ℝ _ _ (grad_u x) (grad_w x))
        (volume.restrict Ω) :=
    hu.add h_two_inner
  have h_assoc :
      (fun x => ‖grad_u x‖ ^ 2 + 2 * @inner ℝ _ _ (grad_u x) (grad_w x) + ‖grad_w x‖ ^ 2) =
        fun x => (‖grad_u x‖ ^ 2 + 2 * @inner ℝ _ _ (grad_u x) (grad_w x)) + ‖grad_w x‖ ^ 2 := by
    funext x
    ring
  rw [h_assoc, integral_add h_first_two hw, integral_add hu h_two_inner,
    integral_const_mul, horth]
  ring

end FormalIslands
formal_artifact.faithfulness_notes: Accepted as a narrower concrete local core in the same ambient setting; it should support the parent node rather than count as full-node certification.

edges.label: main step
edges.explanation: The theorem follows by applying the energy identity to \(w=v-u\) and then using nonnegativity of \(\int_\Omega |\nabla(v-u)|^2\,dx\).
